condensed_reasoning:
## Research

I framed this as a boundary-timing question: does GPT-5.6 reach general availability by **23:59 UTC July 11, 2026**, roughly **11 days** out from my forecast date? I extracted the key facts from the question — GPT-5.6 (`Sol`, `Terra`, `Luna`) was announced **June 26, 2026** as a deliberately staggered, customer-by-customer, government-coordinated limited preview, with GA promised "in the coming weeks" and no calendar date, and independent estimates straddling the deadline.

I ran one `web_search` to confirm there was no newer signal, which surfaced OpenAI's own preview post, the deployment-safety system card, and several news pieces (VentureBeat, Axios, Constellation Research). I then fetched two articles for concrete timeline detail:

- **explainx.ai**: reported that per Axios, Altman told staff he hoped for GA "a couple of weeks later" than June 26 (**~July 10**), gave a best-case window of **mid-July (July 10–17)**, an outer bound before Aug 1, and flagged government-clearance delays, METR safety concerns, and no public waitlist.
- **constellationr.com**: confirmed OpenAI plans GA "in the coming weeks," framed the government gating as a "short-term step," and documented that Anthropic's **Fable 5 / Mythos 5 were pulled back / sidelined** by the US government — a direct delay precedent.

## Key Evidence

- **Against YES:** Government-clearance delay risk is real (Anthropic pullback precedent); the rollout is deliberately staggered and customer-by-customer; "coming weeks" (plural) leans toward the later part of the window; Altman's optimistic ~July 10 target sits right at the boundary and such targets slip.
- **For YES:** The bar is deliberately **low** — an official "rolling out to all of a tier" post on any ONE of ChatGPT/API/Codex counts even if staggered; and OpenAI has strong momentum, explicitly calling the gating temporary.

## Conclusion

I reasoned (rather than formally simulated) that the GA-date distribution likely centers **~July 13–17**, placing the July 11 deadline at only the early tail (~25–35th percentile). I set six factors summing to logit −0.75, landing a tentative logit of **−0.85 (~30%)**. I ran a `reflection` (factor-implied probability 0.32, aligned) and a `premortem` constructing the strongest YES case (multiple independent product/tier paths to a qualifying announcement), which returned an **approve** verdict with no hallucinations. I submitted a final forecast of **0.30 (NO more likely)**.

capability_gaps: No date-distribution / threshold-probability helper to formalize the 'deadline at early tail' intuition into a computed percentile.

classification.approach: Qualitative synthesis of news reporting on the announced GA timeline, converting an estimated GA-date distribution into a threshold-crossing probability via reasoning.
classification.key_uncertainty: Whether government clearance / METR review slips broad GA past July 11, versus OpenAI issuing a qualifying 'available to all of a tier' announcement on any one product before the deadline.

risk_flags.evidence: No prediction-market check (Polymarket/Kalshi/Manifold) or Metaculus community prior was attempted. The agent noted 'No Metaculus CP available (AIB tournament)' but did not look for any external market on this widely-covered launch, which could have anchored the timing estimate.

The core July 10-17 window and Altman '~July 10' target derive from a single reporting chain (Axios, relayed via secondary blogs explainx.ai and constellationr.com). The agent treated the distribution as centering July 13-17 without independent corroboration of the mid-window skew, yet assigned fairly firm 0.7 confidences to the timing-delay factors.

strengths: Correctly identified the crux as a threshold-crossing problem: the deadline (July 11) sits at the early edge of the July 10-17 GA-date cluster, not at its center, which drives the sub-50% estimate.
Surfaced and weighted the Anthropic Fable5/Mythos5 pullback as a concrete government-clearance delay precedent rather than treating 'coming weeks' at face value.
Correctly did NOT treat the June 26 limited government preview as resolving YES, respecting the resolution criteria's exclusion of the gated preview.
Balanced the delay risk against the genuinely low YES bar (any one of ChatGPT/API/Codex; phased 'all-tier' post counts), giving positive-logit factors real weight rather than a token gesture.
Efficient trace: 3 search/fetch calls plus reflection and premortem produced a well-grounded, internally consistent forecast with no wasted steps.
weaknesses: Relied on reasoning rather than an explicit date-distribution model; a lightweight parametric or Monte Carlo model over the July 10-17 window (with a delay tail) would have made the ~25-35th-percentile claim falsifiable rather than asserted.
Did not seek any prediction market or independent quantitative anchor for the GA date; all timing evidence traces back to one Axios-sourced chain via secondary blogs.
Did not probe whether a 'rolling out to all of a tier' announcement had ALREADY appeared in early news for API/Codex, which given the broad YES definition could materially move the estimate.
